channel.title: Github-Copilot on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Github-Copilot on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Copilot Chat now sees your agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on the web now has a better handoff to Copilot cloud agent sessions. It also adds the ability to search and query past agent sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent mode for GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Agent mode is now in public preview in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS). It can help investigate performance issues, tune queries, review maintenance and configuration, identify security concerns, troubleshoot errors, and assist with operational tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Claude Fable 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub describes it as a Mythos-class model aimed at long-horizon, autonomous coding and knowledge-work tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-4.1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-4.1 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on June 1, 2026. The affected surface areas include Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Github Copilot modernization
item.description:
GitHub Copilot modernization agent is now generally available. It provides coordinated orchestration from estate to code to help scale application assessments and upgrades across an app portfolio.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks Lakebase branching with GitHub Copilot agent mode
item.description:
Azure Databricks Lakebase now supports creating copy-on-write branches of a production database with a single command, then connecting GitHub Copilot agent mode to the branch endpoint for debugging AI apps and agents against production data. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now generally available includes Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It supports guided generation of REST, GraphQL, and Model Context Protocol back ends in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description:
GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension is now generally available in Visual Studio Code. It adds AI-assisted capabilities to the visual schema design experience.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Expanded technical preview availability for the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub has expanded the technical preview of the Copilot app to all existing Copilot Pro, Pro+, Business, and Enterprise customers. The app is available for download on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now generally available. It provides a stable API and production-ready support for embedding Copilot’s agentic engine into applications, services, and developer tools.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cloud and local sandboxes for GitHub Copilot now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports secure, isolated sandboxes for tool execution in both local and cloud environments, available in public preview. The update is intended to isolate Copilot actions from the rest of the system.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is now in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is in technical preview, adding on-demand pull request reviews inside Azure DevOps. It must be enabled before use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI and agentic capabilities enhancements in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added Copilot CLI support to Copilot for JetBrains IDEs and expanded agentic capabilities in the IDE integration. The update focuses on new Copilot CLI session features plus additional workflow improvements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini models in Copilot CLI, cloud agent, and the Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub added Gemini 3.1 Pro (Preview) and Gemini 3.5 Flash to more Copilot surfaces. The models are now available in Copilot CLI, Copilot cloud agent, and the GitHub Copilot app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Eclipse: BYOK, skills, and chat updates
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Eclipse adds a refreshed chat experience, more session context visibility, BYOK for Business and Enterprise, improved ABAP support, and skills-related updates. The changelog notes multiple chat and workflow changes, but the provided excerpt does not include full technical details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Evaluation models in auto for individual plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now includes evaluation models for individual non-enterprise users, and these models may be selected by Copilot auto model selection. Users can disable evaluation-model usage in Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.8 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved code understanding and generation compared with earlier models.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: commandline: Disposable agents, durable memory: The architecture behind Squad
item.description:
The article describes Squad, a file-backed agent-team architecture built around human oversight. It emphasizes inspectable memory, explicit orchestration, and moving governance rules from prompts into code.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: github: Target Copilot models to organizations with model rules
item.description:
GitHub added targeted Copilot model rules for enterprise owners. This lets them control which Copilot models are available to each organization, instead of applying the same model availability across the whole enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Doing More with GitHub Copilot as a .NET Developer
item.description:
The post is a brief .NET Blog entry about getting more out of GitHub Copilot as a .NET developer. It points readers to easy ways to start using Copilot more effectively, but provides no technical details in the excerpt.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is now open source. The code is available on GitHub under the MIT license.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Auto model selection now routes based on your task in VS Code
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection in VS Code now routes tasks to the best model based on utilization and model health metrics. GitHub says this is intended to improve quality, reliability, and token efficiency.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.5 Flash is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says it is rolling out broadly and positions it as a Flash-tier model with near-Pro coding quality, but the note does not include technical details beyond that.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent: Fast, cost-efficient models for simple tasks
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent now supports additional models, letting users choose fast, cost-efficient options for simpler tasks. The change expands the set of models available when delegating work to the cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask questions in context with Copilot on web
item.description:
Copilot chat on the web now opens directly on the page you’re viewing, so you can ask questions in context with less switching between pages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions now generally available on mobile, web, and VS Code
item.description:
Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions is now generally available on GitHub Mobile and github.com, with support also referenced for VS Code. It lets you start work in the terminal and continue managing the session from mobile or web.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now the base model for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is now the default base model for all Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organizations, replacing GPT-4.1. GitHub says the base model is used when an organization has not selected another model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory supports user preferences for Pro, Pro+ users
item.description:
Copilot Memory now supports user-level preferences in early access for Copilot Pro and Pro+ users. Copilot can store stated or inferred personal preferences about how you like to work.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app is now available in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is available in technical preview as a GitHub-native desktop experience for starting agentic development from existing work while keeping it isolated and steerable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent supports auto model selection
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now supports Copilot auto model selection. Choosing Auto in the model picker lets Copilot pick the best available model based on system health and model availability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI agent and unified sessions view in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs now includes the Copilot CLI agent and a unified sessions view. The sessions view shows live status for running and queued sessions, and the update also adds an ask… feature.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent now supports more flexible handling of secrets and variables when you delegate tasks to it. The agent runs in a GitHub Actions-backed development environment, and users can pass secrets and variables into that environment.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-4.1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate GPT-4.1 across all Copilot experiences on 2026-06-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Sonnet 4 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Claude Sonnet 4 across all Copilot experiences on May 6, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex
item.description:
GitHub says it will deprecate GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex across GitHub Copilot experiences, including Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions. The notice says GPT-5.2-Codex is excepted in Copilot, but no replacement or timeline details are included in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — April update
item.description:
The April 2026 GitHub Copilot update for Visual Studio focuses on agentic workflows. It adds direct cloud agent sessions from the IDE, user-level support for custom agents, and a new Debugger agent for validation.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent startup is now over 20% faster. The improvement comes from optimized runner environments built with GitHub Actions custom images.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review will start consuming GitHub Actions minutes on June 1, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review will begin consuming GitHub Actions minutes starting June 1, 2026. The change applies to code review usage on pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.5 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed the model performs best on complex, multi-step agentic coding tasks and real-world problem solving.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Inline agent mode in preview and more in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs adds inline agent mode in preview, along with improvements to Next Edit Suggestions and new controls for approving terminal commands and file edits. The update also introduces global auto-approve and other related enhancements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat has added richer context and new capabilities for working with diffs and pull requests. The feature is available when you ask questions about a pull request in Copilot Chat.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Better debugging with GitHub Copilot on the web
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on github.com now better handles pasted stack traces, helping users identify the root cause of errors faster. The update improves stack trace recognition reliability in the web experience.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira: Our latest enhancements
item.description:
GitHub says it has added new enhancements to the GitHub Copilot cloud agent for Jira integration. The update focuses on making the integration more powerful and customizable, with more control for teams.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Bring your own language model key in VS Code now available
item.description:
Copilot Business and Enterprise users can now use bring your own language model key (BYOK) in Visual Studio Code. This lets teams use their own API keys to access supported models in VS Code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot plans for individual users. The post says the changes are intended to support service reliability and sustainability, but the provided excerpt does not include the specific plan details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now generally supports Copilot auto model selection for all Copilot plans. When enabled, Copilot picks the most efficient model automatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.7 is generally available
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.7 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved multi-step task performance and more reliable agentic execution.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enable Copilot cloud agent via custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent can now be enabled selectively for specific organizations using custom properties. Before this change, enterprise admins and AI managers could only turn the agent on or off across all organizations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot data residency in US + EU and FedRAMP compliance now available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports data residency in US and EU regions, keeping inference processing and related data within the selected geography. It also adds FedRAMP compliance for US government customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Remote control CLI sessions on web and mobile in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports remote control via copilot --remote, allowing users to monitor and steer a running CLI session from web and mobile. The feature is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro free trials because of increased abuse of the trial system. The change is intended to protect platform integrity for legitimate developers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description:
VS Code moved to weekly stable releases, and this changelog covers Copilot-related updates in VS Code versions 1.111 through 1.115 shipped in March and early April 2026. The post says the highlights include Autopilot for fully automated workflows, but the provided excerpt does not include the detailed release items.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description:
The post introduces GitHub Copilot Modernization and focuses on the assessment report as the source of truth for cloud modernization work. It frames the report as part of the migration process and positions it as the primary reference for modernization planning.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level runner controls for Copilot cloud agent development environments. Copilot cloud agent tasks now start on GitHub Actions, and orgs can control which runners are used instead of relying only on the default GitHub-hosted runner.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini across all GitHub Copilot experiences on April 1, 2026. The change applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now in public preview. It provides building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities into applications, workflows, and platform services.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization reports now include per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity. This completes CLI metrics coverage across enterprise-, user-, and organization-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio got extensibility updates in the March 2026 release, including custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that improve agent capabilities.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot organization custom instructions are generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization custom instructions are now generally available. Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise org admins can set default instructions for their organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student users through Copilot auto model selection. It is included in Auto for GitHub Copilot Chat in supported clients such as Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Claude Sonnet 4 in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Claude Sonnet 4 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on 2026-05-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3 Pro deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated Gemini 3 Pro across all GitHub Copilot experiences as of March 26, 2026. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements
item.description:
GitHub announced enhancements to the public preview of GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira based on early customer feedback. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active Copilot coding agent users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot coding agent (CCA) activity. Enterprise and organization admins can use this to identify active CCA users by day and other usage views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse
item.description:
Gemini 3.1 Pro is now in public preview for Copilot Enterprise, Business, Pro, and Pro+ users. It can be accessed in GitHub Copilot Chat and in JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Ten Months with Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime
item.description:
The .NET team reports on ten months of using GitHub Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime and summarizes lessons from cloud-AI-assisted development. The post is a data-driven retrospective rather than a feature release.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now resolve auto model selection to actual models
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics now map activity that was previously shown as “Auto” to the actual model used when auto model selection is enabled. This gives clearer model-level reporting in Copilot usage data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Monitor Copilot coding agent logs live in Raycast
item.description:
GitHub says the Copilot extension for Raycast can now be used to monitor Copilot coding agent logs live. The post links Raycast’s launcher and extension support with GitHub Copilot agent log viewing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public preview: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds a public preview of GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer. This expands the visual schema design workflow with AI-assisted help based on developer feedback.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is now generally available in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) 22. It adds AI-powered assistance inside SSMS, including natural-language help to develop, explain, and fix SQL-related tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It provides a guided experience for generating backend APIs directly in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex long-term support in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub introduced GPT-5.3-Codex as a long-term support (LTS) model for GitHub Copilot, aimed at giving enterprises a stable option for internal security and safety reviews. The LTS commitment is available for Copilot Business and Copilot plans.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available in GitHub Copilot and is rolling out to users. GitHub says early tests show it as OpenAI’s highest-performing mini agentic coding model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity. Organization admins can view CLI-specific activity and usage totals in 1-day usage metrics, completing telemetry coverage across enterprise, user, and organization levels.

  • Source: GitHub
